chapter_title.ar: بَابُ دُخُولِ الْمَدِينَةِ وَ زِيَارَةِ النَّبِيِّ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) وَ الدُّعَاءِ عِنْدَ قَبْرِهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 216 – Entering Al-Medina and Ziyarah of the Prophet<sup>saww</sup>, and the supplication at his<sup>saww</sup> grave

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/264)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/264)
text: حَضَرْتُ أَبَا الْحَسَنِ الْأَوَّلَ ( عليه السلام ) وَ هَارُونَ الْخَلِيفَةَ وَ عِيسَى بْنَ جَعْفَرٍ وَ جَعْفَرَ بْنَ يَحْيَى بِالْمَدِينَةِ قَدْ جَاءُوا إِلَى قَبْرِ النَّبِيِّ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) فَقَالَ هَارُونُ لِأَبِي الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) تَقَدَّمْ فَأَبَى فَتَقَدَّمَ هَارُونُ فَسَلَّمَ وَ قَامَ نَاحِيَةً

وَ قَالَ عِيسَى بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ لِأَبِي الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) تَقَدَّمْ فَأَبَى فَتَقَدَّمَ عِيسَى فَسَلَّمَ وَ وَقَفَ مَعَ هَارُونَ فَقَالَ جَعْفَرٌ لِأَبِي الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) تَقَدَّمْ فَأَبَى فَتَقَدَّمَ جَعْفَرٌ فَسَلَّمَ وَ وَقَفَ مَعَ هَارُونَ
وَ تَقَدَّمَ أَبُو الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) فَقَالَ السَّلَامُ عَلَيْكَ يَا أَبَهْ أَسْأَلُ اللَّهَ الَّذِي اصْطَفَاكَ وَ اجْتَبَاكَ وَ هَدَاكَ وَ هَدَى بِكَ أَنْ يُصَلِّيَ عَلَيْكَ
فَقَالَ هَارُونُ لِعِيسَى سَمِعْتَ مَا قَالَ قَالَ نَعَمْ فَقَالَ هَارُونُ أَشْهَدُ أَنَّهُ أَبُوهُ حَقّاً

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Ali Bin Hassan, from one of our companions who said,

‘I was present with Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup> the 1<sup>st</sup> (7<sup>th</sup> Imam<sup>asws</sup>), and Haroun (Al Rasheed) the Caliph, and Isa Bin Ja’far, and Ja’far Bin Yahya, at Al-Medina, and they had come to the grave of the Prophet<sup>saww</sup>. So Haroun said to Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup>, ‘Proceed!’ But, he<sup>asws</sup> refused. So Haroun proceeded and greeted, and stood nearby.

And Isa Bin ja’far said to Abu Al Hassan<sup>asws</sup>, ‘Proceed’. But he<sup>asws</sup> refused. So Isa proceeded and greeted, and paused alongside Haroun. So Ja’far said to Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup>, ‘Proceed’. But he<sup>asws</sup> refused. So Ja’far proceeded and paused alongside Haroun.

And Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup> proceeded, so he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘The greetings be upon you<sup>saww</sup>, O father<sup>saww</sup>! I<sup>asws</sup> ask Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Who Chose you<sup>saww</sup>, and Appointed you<sup>saww</sup>, and Guided you<sup>saww</sup>, and Guided through you<sup>saww</sup>, that He<sup>azwj</sup> should Send Blessings upon you<sup>saww</sup>’.
So Haroun said to Isa, ‘Did you hear what he<sup>asws</sup> said?’ He said, ‘Yes’. So Haroun said, ‘I testify that he<sup>saww</sup> is his<sup>asws</sup> father<sup>saww</sup>, truly’.
en.sarwar: 8. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from Ali ibn Hassan from certain individuals of our people who have said the following: “I was present when abu al-Hassan the 1st, Harun, the Caliph, ‘Isa ibn Ja’far and Ja’far ibn Yahya came to al-Madinah to visit the grave of the Holy Prophet, O Allah, grant compensation to Muhammad and his family worthy of their services to Your cause. Harun said to abu al-Hassan, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, ‘You go first.’ He (the Imam) abstained. Harun then moved forward, offered al- Salam and then moved to one side. ‘Isa ibn Ja’far then said to abu al-Hassan, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, ‘You go first.’ He (the Imam) abstained. ‘Isa then moved forward and offered al-Salam and then moved to one side alongside Harun. Ja’far then said to abu al-Hassan, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, ‘You go first.’ He (the Imam) abstained. Ja’far then moved forward, offered al-Salam, and then moved to one side alongside Harun. Abu al-Hassan, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, then moved forward and said, ‘(I hereby declare, affirm and acknowledge that you are a ‘Alayhi al-Salam and my) submission to (your commandments) O my father. I appeal before Allah, who chose you, selected you, guided you and guided through you to grant salawat (favors and compensation to you worthy of Your services to Allah’s cause).’ Harun said to ‘Isa, ‘Did you hear what he said?’ He replied, ‘Yes, I heard what he said.’ Harun then said, ‘I testify that he (the Messenger of Allah) is indeed his father.’”
